page.title: Rules
page.subtitle: The authoritative reference for Cycling Zone's limits, rates and formulas.
page.intro: This page is the source of truth for the game's numbers. The Help page explains how to do things; this page lists what the limits and rates actually are. Where a number is set by an admin (auction times, emergency-loan rates) it can change between seasons, and those are noted as such.
page.lastVerified: Numbers verified against the game code.

cap.title: Squad cap
cap.text: You can hold a maximum of {squadCap} riders, the same in every division. There is no minimum: you can sell down to 0 riders without penalty.

enforcement.title: Hard cap
enforcement.text: The cap is a hard limit. You cannot buy, bid or loan past your division's maximum of {squadCap} riders. There is no buffer, no fine and no auto-sale: a deal that would take you over the cap is simply rejected.

academyExempt.title: Academy riders are separate
academyExempt.text: Academy riders never count towards the {squadCap}-rider senior cap and are never auto-sold by squad enforcement. They have their own 8-place academy cap.

startingBalance.title: Starting balance
startingBalance.text: Every team starts with {startingBalance} CZ$. CZ$ is the in-game currency, earned by playing and never bought with real money.

sponsor.title: Sponsor income
sponsor.text: Season-1 sponsor income is scaled by division so a competent team is sustainable: Division 1 {sponsorD1} CZ$, Division 2 {sponsorD2} CZ$, Division 3 {sponsorD3} CZ$. The final payout is capped at {sponsorCeilingS1} CZ$ in season 1 and {sponsorCeilingS2} CZ$ from season 2 onwards (after board modifiers).

upkeep.title: Upkeep
upkeep.text: Each season your team pays a fixed operating cost (upkeep) at season start, scaled by division so higher divisions pay more. The exact figures are a balance lever the developer tunes between seasons; you always see the amount booked in your Finance log as 'upkeep'.

salary.title: Rider salary
salary.text: A rider's salary is frozen when you sign them at {salaryRatePct}% of their market value at that moment, and does not change automatically afterwards. Academy riders use a separate youth rate (see Academy).

negativeInterest.title: Negative-balance interest
negativeInterest.text: If your balance is negative at season start, {negativeInterestPct}% interest is added on top of the shortfall.

debtCeiling.title: Debt ceiling
debtCeiling.text: Each division has a maximum debt: Division 1 {debtD1} CZ$, Division 2 {debtD2} CZ$, Division 3 {debtD3} CZ$. New loans are rejected if the loan plus its fee would push you over the ceiling. Automatic emergency loans can exceed it, but you get a warning and must cut costs.

emergencyLoan.title: Emergency loans
emergencyLoan.text: Emergency loans carry a fee and interest set in the loan configuration (currently around 15% each). These rates are admin-configurable and may change; the loan form on the Finance page always shows the current numbers.

divisionBonus.title: Division bonus (season end)
divisionBonus.text: A one-time bonus paid at season end based on your final placing in your division:

duration.title: Auction length
duration.text: An auction runs for {auctionActiveHours, plural, one {# active hour} other {# active hours}}. Night hours don't count: trading is active 08:00–24:00 every day (paused 00:00–08:00). These windows are admin-configurable.

minBid.title: Minimum bid
minBid.text: Your bid must be at least {minBidStep} CZ$ above the current price. If no one has bid yet, you can match the asking price.

extension.title: Last-minute extension
extension.text: A bid placed in the final {auctionExtensionMinutes} minutes extends the auction so others get a chance to respond. No sniping in the last second.

proxy.title: Auto-bidding & reserved balance
proxy.text: You can set an auto-bid cap and the system bids up to it on your behalf. Active auctions where you lead count as reserved balance and a reserved squad slot at the same time, so you can't overcommit beyond your available funds.

contractInherited.title: Contracts transfer as-is
contractInherited.text: When a rider changes team (by transfer, swap or auction sale) the buyer takes over the existing contract unchanged. Salary and contract length are never recalculated on a deal.

loansCount.title: Loans count towards the cap
loansCount.text: Active loan deals count towards your squad size, so a full squad can be rejected on loans, auctions or other incoming deals.

prize.title: Prize money
prize.text: Prize money is paid per result: points for the placing × {prizePerPoint} CZ$. Only races with a race class (Tour de France, WorldTour, ProSeries, etc.) generate prize money; races without a class give 0.

resultType.title: Result type
resultType.text: A one-day race's result counts as a general-classification (GC) result. The result type is only a label: prize money depends on the points table for the placing, not the type.

freeAgentPrize.title: Riders without a team
freeAgentPrize.text: Prize money earned by riders who aren't on a manager team raises their market value but is never paid out to anyone. Unowned riders get more valuable, but no team receives the cash.

structure.title: Division structure
structure.text: There are {divisions} divisions. Division {minDivision} is the top; Division {maxDivision} is the bottom. Each upper division targets {divisionCapacity} real teams; the bottom division is overflow and can grow past that.

promotion.title: Promotion & relegation
promotion.text: Promotion and relegation start from season {firstPromotionSeason}. Seasons 1 and 2 end without division changes to let the league settle first. From then on, the top teams move up and the bottom teams move down at season end.

transition.title: Manual season change
transition.text: Moving to a new season is a deliberate manual admin action, never an automatic cron. Window-close, the final whistle and squad checks remain automatic.

slots.title: Academy size
slots.text: Your academy holds up to {academySlots} young riders (a hard cap), separate from your senior squad.

age.title: Age range
age.text: Academy riders are aged {academyMinAge}–{academyMaxAge}. Younger prospects get a youth multiplier on development that tapers towards age 22.

salary.title: Youth salary & contracts
salary.text: Academy riders are paid {academySalaryPct}% of their market value and sign {academyContractLength}-season youth contracts.

drift.title: Upkeep (drift)
drift.text: Each filled academy place costs {academyDrift} CZ$ per season, charged at season start. A small academy (3–4 places) is comfortably affordable in every division; a full 8-place academy is a deliberate, heavier investment.

starProtection.title: Star-rider protection
starProtection.text: A rider whose market value is at least {starValue} CZ$ counts as a star and is protected from forced sales. This is the same value you see in the rider's profile.

noBoardForBots.title: AI, bank & frozen teams
noBoardForBots.text: AI teams, the bank and frozen teams never get board features; those are manager-only. They also never trigger board consequences.

academyDisabledNote: The Academy launches with the relaunch. The limits below are final and shown for reference; the feature isn't live yet.

faqLinks.prizeMoneyFaq: What is prize money, and when is it paid out?
